title: Mazicia Organa

|death=Between [[38 BBY/Legends|38]] and [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]<ref name="Dates">According to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'', the events of the novel ''[[The Clone Wars: Wild Space]]'' took place in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. In the novel, [[Tayvor Mandirly]]'s [[Death/Legends|death]] was documented as having happened sixteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] earlier.</ref>

Since he is not a [[Hybrid/Legends|hybrid]], both his parents were necessarily Human.</ref>

*[[High Court of Alderaan]]<ref name="High Court">In the [[Star Wars (radio)|radio dramatization]] of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], it was stated that the [[High Court of Alderaan|High Court]] that presided over the [[High Council of Alderaan]] was composed of the royal family. Even though Mazicia Organa's connection to the High Court was not made explicit in any source, she was, as the [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], automatically affiliated with it.</ref>

{{Quote|She never got over what they did to him. In the end, his death killed her.|Bail Organa|<ref name="Wild Space" />}}
'''Mazicia Organa''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who served as the [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] during the period leading up to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a pan-[[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] conflict. She was also the [[Parent/Legends|mother]] of [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Prestor Organa]], who became the last [[Viceroy/Legends|Viceroy]] and [[First Chairman/Legends|First Chairman]] of the [[Alderaan system/Legends|Alderaan system]]. In [[38 BBY/Legends|38 BBY]], the life of Mazicia Organa was shattered when her youngest brother, the [[Agricultural expert|agricultural specialist]] [[Tayvor Mandirly]], was [[Death/Legends|murdered]] on a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] known as [[Antar 4/Legends|Antar 4]]. The famous [[blockade runner]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] later known as the [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']] was built during her reign, under the name ''Star of Alderaan''. Despite the [[Time/Legends|time]] that had gone by since Mandirly's passing, the mourning Queen was never able to resume a normal life. At some point before the outbreak of the Clone Wars in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the Queen's sorrow was such that she died of grief.

==Biography==
[[File:Interrupt_Bail_Organa.png|thumb|left|150px|Viceroy Bail Organa, Queen Mazicia's son]]
A [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="Species" /> [[Gender/Legends|female]], Mazicia Organa came from [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]],<ref name="SWI90" /> a pristine [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of refined culture,<ref name="ISWU">''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]''</ref> in the waning [[Time/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="SWI90" /> She was one of several [[Youngling/Legends|children]], the youngest of which became the renowned [[agricultural expert]] [[Tayvor Mandirly]].<ref name="Wild Space" /> Later in life, Mazicia joined the [[House of Organa/Legends|House of Organa]],<ref name="Organa">According to ''[[Star Wars Blueprints: Rebel Edition]]'', [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa's]] [[Parent/Legends|mother]] bore the name "[[House of Organa/Legends|Organa]]" [[Gender/Legends|herself]]. In the novel ''[[The Clone Wars: Wild Space]]'', the Queen's brother [[Tayvor Mandirly|Mandirly]] had the last name "Mandirly." This implies that Mazicia took the name of "Organa" by [[Marriage/Legends|marrying]] Bail's father.</ref> one of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] most respected [[Ancient Houses]],<ref name="Children of the Jedi">[[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']]</ref> by [[Marriage/Legends|marrying]] the [[Viceroy/Legends|Viceroy]] [[Organa (viceroy)|Organa]].<ref name="DFR">''[[Dark Force Rising]]''</ref> Eventually, Mazicia Organa was [[Crown/Legends|crowned]] [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] of Alderaan.<ref name="SWI90" /> In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[67 BBY/Legends|67 BBY]],<ref name="FF140">{{FactFile|140|FAM 1-2|Skywalker Family Tree}}</ref> she [[Reproduction/Legends|gave birth]] to a baby boy, the [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Prestor Organa]].<ref name="SWB:RE" /> Together, the Queen and the Viceroy Organa became dedicated and loving [[Parent/Legends|parents]], but they never spoiled their son, even though his [[Noble/Legends|patrician]] education came with many privileges.<ref name="Wild Space" />

In [[38 BBY/Legends|38 BBY]],<ref name="Dates" /> Mazicia's brother Tayvor was [[Death/Legends|murdered]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Antar 4/Legends|Antar 4]] by [[Raxis]] and [[Nolid]], two members of a local [[Unidentified criminal organization|criminal organization]], during a [[colfillini]] plantation dispute on the planet. Mandirly's charred body was found nailed to a stake by the [[Padawan/Legends|Jedi apprentice]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. That traumatic event was devastating for the Queen of Alderaan, and from then on, sorrow began to take over her life.<ref name="Wild Space" />

Sometime before [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]],<ref name="Agrippa">The reference book ''[[Star Wars Blueprints: Rebel Edition]]'' states that the construction of the [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Star of Alderaan'']] took place when [[Agrippa Aldrete/Legends|Agrippa Aldrete]] was still a [[Senator/Legends|Senator]]. According to the novel ''[[Cloak of Deception]]'', Aldrete had already been replaced by [[Bail Antilles/Legends|Bail Antilles]] for an indeterminate period of [[Time/Legends|time]] as of [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]]. Therefore, the construction must have happened before that [[Standard year/Legends|year]].</ref> the Queen ordered the construction of the [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Star of Alderaan'']], a [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] the use of which was reserved to [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Agrippa Aldrete/Legends|Agrippa Aldrete]],<ref name="SWI90" /> who represented the [[Alderaan sector/Legends|Alderaan sector]] in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]].<ref name="db">{{DB|character|aldrete|Aldrete, Agrippa}}</ref> Organa allowed the ship to serve the House of Alderaan without granting it the status of [[Royal Alderaan Civil Fleet/Legends|royal vessel]] so that it could have additional defensive systems and weapons added, predicting that her family might someday need more protection in dangerous situations.<ref name="SWI90" /> As years went by, Mazicia never managed to get over her brother's death, which eventually led to her own demise. When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist Confederacy]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Queen Mazicia Organa had been dead for some [[Time/Legends|time]], and the throne had been passed to [[Breha Organa/Legends|Breha Antilles]], who was the wife of Bail Organa.<ref name="Wild Space" />

===Legacy===
[[File:TantiveIVToydaria-SL.png|thumb|right|200px|The ''Star of Alderaan'', later renamed ''Tantive IV'', was commissioned by Queen Mazicia Organa.]]
Long after Queen Mazicia's death, her memory was still vivid in the mind of her son. At the start of the Clone Wars, Bail Organa was still deeply resentful against Raxis and Nolid, believing they should have been executed for provoking his mother's passing.<ref name="Wild Space" /> House Organa also continued to use the ''Star of Alderaan'', later renamed the ''Tantive IV'', for over three decades,<ref name="SWB:RE" /> and their involvement in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]] against the newly [[Declaration of a New Order|formed]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="TFUG">''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]''</ref> proved Mazicia's prediction that her family might need more protection.<ref name="SWI90" />

==Personality and traits==
The Queen of Alderaan Mazicia Organa was deeply attached to her family. Her son Bail would remember her as a "doting" mother, and the Queen's love for her brother ultimately sent her into depression after he died.<ref name="Wild Space" /> During her reign, however, Organa proved to be far-sighted and proactive. She was capable of foreseeing the [[Imperial Era/Legends|dark times]] to come, providing her descendants with a private starship that could be fitted for war.<ref name="SWI90" />

==Behind the scenes==
Mazicia Organa was first mentioned in the [[2006]] [[Star Wars Insider 90|October issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine, in the article "20 Things You Didn't Know About the ''Tantive IV''." The article was written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] as part of the ''[[Refresher Reading]]'' recurring feature. In the text, Mazicia Organa was only referred to as a past Queen of Alderaan, with no explicit mention of her family ties with Bail Organa.<ref name="SWI90" /> [[2008|Two years later]], Bail Organa's mother was mentioned, albeit unnamed, in the novel ''[[The Clone Wars: Wild Space]]'', written by [[Karen Miller]].<ref name="Wild Space" /> The two characters were confirmed to be one and the same on page 15 of the [[2010]] reference book ''[[Star Wars Blueprints: Rebel Edition]]''.<ref name="SWB:RE" />
